body.conclusion: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Pebble & Ecosystem maintenance: TypeScript core of Cardano" Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action Constitutional.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A reviewer might argue that disclosing prior treasury receipts by reference to an externally linked IPFS document, rather than enumerating prior amounts and governance action IDs inline, makes verification harder for a voter reading only the on-chain record. The Constitution requires disclosure of whether the recipient has received ADA from the Treasury within the last 24 months; it does not prescribe format. The retrospective document is IPFS-anchored and therefore immutable and retrievable. The disclosure requirement is met.

A reviewer might further argue that embedding the audit cost within FTE operational overhead, without stating a discrete ADA amount allocated to audits, makes it impossible to confirm that a meaningful sum has been set aside. The Constitution requires an allocation of ADA to cover periodic independent audits; it does not require a separately enumerated line item or minimum percentage. The audit is described in full: scope (quarterly ex-post verification), frequency (quarterly reviews and a final assessment), independence (third party with no operational involvement), and basis (verifiable transaction-level evidence and financial summaries). The requirement is met.

A reviewer might also note, as a policy observation rather than a constitutional finding, that pricing FTEs in USD and converting at a fixed $0.25/ADA rate exposes the treasury to exchange-rate risk in both directions: ADA appreciation would deliver more real-world value than intended, while ADA depreciation could leave HLabs under-resourced. This is a market risk, not a constitutional deficiency, and is common to nearly all ADA-denominated treasury proposals in this cycle.
body.precedentDiscussion: The SundaeSwap treasury.ak/vendor.ak custody structure, including auto-abstain delegation and failsafe sweep, was reviewed and accepted in prior rationales under Articles II.7.5 and II.7.6. That finding carries forward. The oversight board composition here differs from the IO proposals (which use Intersect/Sundae Labs), but the constitutional standard is met by any named, independent, non-recipient administrator structure.
body.rationaleStatement:
This Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action requests 4,600,000 ADA to fund two engineering tracks over 12 months (Q2 2026 through Q1 2027): development of the Pebble smart contract language (3.5 FTEs) and maintenance of Harmonic Laboratories' TypeScript protocol library stack through the next hard fork (1.5 FTEs). Harmonic Laboratories is the proposer and recipient. The proposal is not IOG-affiliated. Funds are disbursed milestone-by-milestone through the SundaeSwap treasury.ak/vendor.ak escrow, co-signed by an independent three-member oversight board.

The relevant provisions are Article II.6, Article II.7, and Appendix I TREASURY guardrails.

**Article II.6.1 (Metadata Format).** Metadata is IPFS-anchored at QmYJynuvpv1mY7yWDL763LcscHrFSCAW9d8SvCDf4BRdHK; Koios confirms meta_is_valid true, meaning the on-chain hash matches the document (3f8536caece1c7fed0f1a1d29f2ea893a6a7f592be4f1f906b363dbfdacc6544). Conforms to CIP-0108.

**Article II.6.2 (Metadata Structure).** Title, abstract, motivation, and rationale are present. The rationale includes a full budget breakdown by FTE, a five-milestone disbursement schedule with objective acceptance criteria, an independent oversight board, a constitutionality checklist, a Cardano 2030 KPI alignment table, and a reporting framework. Sufficient.

**Article II.7.1 (Terms of the Withdrawal).** Purpose (Pebble language delivery and TypeScript ecosystem maintenance), delivery period (Q2 2026 through Q1 2027), itemized costs (5 FTEs at $200k/FTE base, plus 15% contingency, totaling $1,150,000 converted at $0.25/ADA to 4,600,000 ADA), and refund conditions (unused contingency swept to the Cardano Treasury at contract expiration, enforced at the contract level and not overridable) are all stated. Milestone disbursements require independent oversight board co-signature before release. Satisfied.

**Article II.7.2 (Prior Funding Disclosure).** Harmonic Laboratories discloses prior treasury receipts by reference to a 2025 retrospective document anchored on IPFS at QmZVw82XNXNsgGmBj39R26Mx7jgzWaNjSw4A7JM9Erye9c. The disclosure is present. The format differs from proposals that enumerate prior amounts and governance action IDs inline; here the detail is externalized to a linked document. The requirement to disclose is satisfied.

**Article II.7.3 (Net Change Limit Compliance).** The 350,000,000 ADA Net Change Limit (epochs 613-713) applies. The 4,600,000 ADA ask is well within it.

**Article II.7.4 (Audits and Oversight).** A periodic independent financial audit is incorporated within the FTE cost structure's operational overhead. The audit covers quarterly ex-post verification of disbursements, traceability of fund movements, and alignment between milestone-based disbursements and project-level financial reporting. The audit is conducted by an independent third party with no operational involvement in HLabs, with a final assessment at close. The allocation for independent audits is embedded within the FTE overhead rather than stated as a discrete ADA amount, but the scope, frequency, and independence requirements are fully described. In addition, the three-member oversight board reviews and co-signs each milestone before disbursement, monthly progress updates are published publicly, and a public transaction journal records every on-chain action. The audit requirement and the oversight metrics requirement are satisfied.

**Article II.7.5 (Administrator).** An independent oversight board is named: Santiago Carmuega (TxPipe, Dolos), Lucas Rosa (Aiken, Starstream, Midnight), and Chris Gianelloni (BlinkLabs, Dingo). The proposal confirms none has a stake in HLabs. Disbursements require HLabs initiation plus any one board member co-signature; any single board member can pause a milestone; structural changes require board majority. Satisfied.

**Article II.7.6 (Custody).** Funds are held in the SundaeSwap treasury.ak contract until vendor.ak disbursement co-signing occurs. Auto-abstain DRep delegation is enforced at the contract level. No SPO delegation. Unused funds at expiration are swept to the Cardano Treasury by contract enforcement, not by discretion. Satisfied.

**Appendix I (Guardrails).** TREASURY-02a: 4,600,000 ADA does not exceed the Net Change Limit. TREASURY-03a: Withdrawal denominated in ADA. TREASURY-01a: not engaged.
body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Pebble & Ecosystem maintenance: TypeScript core of Cardano" Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action Constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
